title: Maximum Sum Circular Subarray
description:
Given a circular integer array nums of length n, return _the maximum possible sum of a non-empty subarray of_ nums.

A circular array means the end of the array connects to the beginning of the array. Formally, the next element of nums[i] is nums[(i + 1) % n] and the previous element of nums[i] is nums[(i - 1 + n) % n].

A subarray may only include each element of the fixed buffer nums at most once. Formally, for a subarray nums[i], nums[i + 1], ..., nums[j], there does not exist i <= k1, k2 <= j with k1 % n == k2 % n.

solutionCode.python:
```python
from heapq import heappush, heappop

def reachableNodes(edges, maxMoves, n):
    graph = [[] for _ in range(n)]
    for u, v, cnt in edges:
        graph[u].append((v, cnt + 1))
        graph[v].append((u, cnt + 1))

    visited = set()
    pq = [(-maxMoves, 0)]

    while pq:
        moves_left, node = heappop(pq)

        if node in visited:
            continue
        visited.add(node)

        for next_node, moves_needed in graph[node]:
            moves_left_after = moves_left - moves_needed
            if next_node not in visited and moves_left_after > 0:
                heappush(pq, (moves_left_after, next_node))

    return len(visited)
```

    
    The problem can be solved using Dijkstra's algorithm.

1. First, convert the given graph into an adjacency list (graph) so that it stores the adjacent nodes and the edge weights between them.
2. Initialize a set (visited) for storing the visited nodes.
3. Initialize a max-heap priority queue (pq), with the initial maximum moves left and the starting node (0) as a tuple.
4. Run a while loop until the priority queue is empty:
   a. Pop the top element from the queue which has the maximum moves left and the corresponding node.
   b. Check if the node is already visited. If yes, continue to the next element in the queue.
   c. If the node is not visited, add it to the visited set.
   d. Loop through each neighbor of the current node:
      i. Calculate moves_left_after: moves left after reaching the neighbor node.
      ii. If the neighbor node is not visited and the moves_left_after is greater than 0, push it into the priority queue.
5. Return the size of the visited set.
